mnemonics: SSC: Static means Shared, Single, Class-level
keyPoints: Static members belong to the class itself, not to individual instances.
Static methods can only directly access other static members (variables and methods).
Static variables are initialized when the class is loaded into memory, before any objects are created.
commonMistakes: Trying to access non-static members directly from a static method (causes a compilation error).
Assuming each object gets its own copy of a static variable (they all share the same one).
